subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 30
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a very strong, prehensile tail and living in trees?
questions.explanation: Many monkeys have tails that can grip branches, which helps them move through the forest canopy.
questions.hint: They are famous for climbing and eating bananas.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their physical traits.

questions.q: What is the capital city of the United States?
questions.explanation: Washington, D.C. is the capital of the United States, home to the White House and the Capitol building.
questions.hint: It is named after the first U.S. President.

questions.q: Which part of the plant produces seeds and attracts pollinators?
questions.explanation: Flowers use bright colors and scents to attract bees and butterflies, which help them produce seeds.
questions.hint: They are often the most colorful part of a plant.

questions.q: What do we call a person who is trained to help sick or injured people?
questions.explanation: A doctor is a professional trained to diagnose, treat, and prevent illnesses and injuries.
questions.hint: They work in hospitals and clinics.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your body?
questions.explanation: Washing your hands kills germs and prevents you from getting sick.
questions.hint: You should do this after playing outside.

questions.q: Which community helper is responsible for protecting our community and enforcing laws?
questions.explanation: Police officers are tasked with maintaining public order and ensuring citizens follow the law.
questions.hint: They wear a uniform and carry a badge.

questions.q: What is the name of the planet we live on?
questions.explanation: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only one known to support life.
questions.hint: It is often called the 'Blue Planet'.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of energy that comes from the wind?
questions.explanation: Wind energy is produced by using large turbines to capture the energy from moving air.

questions.q: What is the freezing point of water in degrees Fahrenheit?
questions.explanation: At sea level, water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit.

questions.q: Which season is known for being the coldest?
questions.explanation: Winter is characterized by the lowest temperatures of the year and often snow or ice.
